title: Wood Pullout Cabinet Drawer Organizer
summary: A shallow pull-out tray on slides that retrofits into an existing base cabinet — turn a deep, awkward shelf into a drawer you can actually reach the back of.
description:
Reaching into the back of a base cabinet is a chore; this fixes it with a shallow wooden tray on drawer slides that rolls the whole shelf out to you. It's a fast, high-impact kitchen upgrade built from a single 1x6, a bit of 2x2, and some scrap plywood, and it slides in without modifying the cabinet permanently.

Because it's a retrofit, everything is cut to fit your cabinet, so the first job is measuring: the clearance opening (remember the door hinges eat into it) and the depth. The source's opening was about 14-1/2" once the hinges were accounted for — measure yours carefully, because a tray built to the nominal opening won't clear the hinges.

Build a simple 1x6 box with a tacked-on 1/4" plywood bottom. The clever part is the 2x2 rails: the drawer slides mount to them, and the 2x2s then either screw to the cabinet shelf from underneath (in a tight cabinet) or sit as a base. Mount the slide pieces level and in the exact same position on both 2x2s — the plan is emphatic about this, because slides that aren't parallel bind. The finished tray lifts out for cleaning, and the 2x2 rails double as a lip that keeps items from sliding off the sides.

tools.note: Optional; 2" countersunk screws also build the box.

cutList.note: Cut to your opening minus slide clearance.

steps.title: Measure the cabinet
steps.body: Measure the clearance opening of your cabinet — remembering the door hinges take up a little room — and the depth. The example had about a 14-1/2" clearance opening once the hinges were accounted for. These numbers set every cut; a tray built to the nominal opening won't clear the hinges.

steps.title: Build the tray box
steps.body: With everything cut, build a simple box — 1-1/4" pocket screws or 2" countersunk screws and glue. Keep it square.

steps.title: Add the bottom
steps.body: Since side/bottom-mount slides carry the load, you can just tack a 1/4" plywood bottom onto the box with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Mount the slides on the 2x2 rails
steps.body: Attach the drawer-slide pieces to the 2x2 rails, mounting them level and in the exact same position on both 2x2s. This is critical — slides that aren't parallel will bind and jam.

steps.title: Install and fit the drawer
steps.body: Position the whole assembly inside the cabinet. In a tight cabinet, screw the 2x2s to the shelf from underneath; with more clearance, fasten them however suits. Slide the tray in — it lifts out easily for cleaning or finishing, and the 2x2 rails keep items from sliding off the sides.

images.alt: Wooden pull-out tray organizer on slides inside a cabinet
